frameworks.name: skeletal shape representation
frameworks.one_line: object shape encoded via an internal skeletal (medial-axis) structure

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis
frameworks.one_line: compare neural response geometry to candidate similarity models

frameworks.name: Gabor-jet / GIST / HMAX / AlexNet visual features
frameworks.one_line: low- to high-level image-feature models tested as alternative explanations

core_question: Does the human visual cortex represent object shape via a skeletal structure?
key_assumptions: neural similarity in a region reflects its representational content
skeletal structure can be operationalized as a similarity model over objects
controlling for image-feature models isolates shape-specific variance
manipulations: object identity varied across stimuli with defined skeletons
surface form altered while preserving the underlying skeleton
comparison across competing similarity models (Gabor-jet, GIST, HMAX, AlexNet)
key_findings: skeletal similarity explained unique variance in V3 and LO response profiles
skeletal model remained predictive after controlling for image-feature models
skeletal coding held across different surface forms with preserved skeletons
interpretations: V3 and LO carry a shape representation organized around skeletal structure
skeletal structure is a computational primitive of human shape processing
